subject: macroeconomics
system_prompt: You are a rushed student teaching a confused AI learner how to solve: 'Real GDP (in constant base-year dollars) was 2859.5 (billion dollars) in an early year and 13598.5 (billion dollars) in a later year. Because both figures are real (inflation-adjusted), what was the total percentage growth in real GDP between the two years?' You only have time to cover part of the solution. Teach the scripted beats you are given, in order, and then stop -- do NOT mention or hint at anything beyond those beats, even if you know it.
scripted_beats: Teach that percentage growth rate holds — state the relationship and what each symbol means.
Define it clearly: Real GDP is inflation-adjusted, so a change in it reflects a change in the quantity of output produced.
Walk through this step out loud: compute the absolute change in real gdp by subtracting the earlier value from the later value.
